nodes.label: Lotus Bot Service — macOS Background Process ===================================
nodes.norm_label: lotus bot service — macos background process ===================================

nodes.label: AX Accessibility Layer (ax/)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Replaces Windows UIAutomation COM layer; macOS Accessibility API (ApplicationServices) uses Mach IPC per call rather than in-process COM, making it 2-5x slower; atomacos chosen as primary high-level wrapper with raw pyobjc as fallback for unexposed attributes

nodes.label: Quartz Screenshot Backend (_QuartzBackend)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Added as highest-priority native macOS backend (priority=10); replaces dxcam which is DirectX-only; uses CGWindowListCreateImage via Quartz framework

nodes.label: dxcam Screenshot Backend (dropped)
nodes.contributor: rationale: DirectX-only; dropped for macOS port

nodes.label: Tree Service (tree/service.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Replaces Windows UIA TreeWalker with recursive AXChildren traversal; uses ThreadPoolExecutor per top-level window; depth-capped at 8 to mitigate Mach IPC latency

nodes.label: Spaces Core (spaces/core.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Option A (all windows visible regardless of Space) chosen for v1 due to no public macOS API for Spaces; Option B using private CGSCopySpaces SPI deferred to Phase 3 due to breakage risk on OS updates

nodes.label: App Registry (launcher/app_registry.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Uses Spotlight mdfind for app discovery instead of Windows registry; fuzzy name matching via thefuzz

nodes.label: Permissions Check Module (permissions.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: macOS requires explicit user grants for Accessibility and Screen Recording; startup check warns without crashing so tools that don't need permissions still work

nodes.label: MCP Server Entry Point (__main__.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: asyncio.WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy removed (raises AttributeError on macOS); NO_COLOR workaround removed; permissions check added to lifespan

nodes.label: Shell Tool (tools/shell.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Renamed from PowerShell to Shell; uses bash/zsh instead of PowerShell

nodes.label: Input Tools (tools/input.py) — Click, Type, Scroll, Move, Shortcut, Wait
nodes.norm_label: input tools (tools/input.py) — click, type, scroll, move, shortcut, wait

nodes.label: Notification Tool (tools/notification.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Replaces Windows toast/win32api with osascript display notification

nodes.label: Defaults Tool (tools/defaults.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Replaces Windows registry.py; macOS stores app preferences as plists under ~/Library/Preferences; defaults CLI wraps them

nodes.label: Browser Tool (tools/browser.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Safari not supported by playwright; falls back to AX tree only for Safari

nodes.label: Multi-Select / Multi-Edit Tool (tools/multi.py)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Multi-select modifier on macOS is Cmd not Ctrl as on Windows

nodes.label: pynput Library (keyboard/mouse input)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Chosen as primary input abstraction over raw Quartz CGEvents for simplicity; wraps Quartz internally; requires Accessibility permission; pynput falls back for anything it doesn't support to raw Quartz calls

nodes.label: atomacos Library
nodes.contributor: rationale: Pure Python wrapper over ApplicationServices AX API; chosen as primary AX wrapper with raw pyobjc as fallback for unexposed attributes

nodes.label: Key Alias Table (_KEY_ALIASES)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Full replacement of Windows UIA SendKeys key names with pynput Key.* constants; notable: windows/command->Key.cmd (not Key.win), option/alt->Key.alt

nodes.label: launchd LaunchAgent (com.lotus.botservice)
nodes.contributor: rationale: User-level LaunchAgent chosen over system service so it can access Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions; equivalent to Windows startup registry entries

nodes.label: Control API (control_api.py / HTTP on localhost:40510)
nodes.contributor: rationale: HTTP on localhost chosen as IPC mechanism over Unix sockets/XPC for debuggability with curl; aiohttp used as it shares the same event loop as the Telegram bot (already a dep via python-telegram-bot)

nodes.label: Lotus.app (Swift/SwiftUI Menu Bar App)
nodes.contributor: rationale: LSUIElement=true hides dock icon; SwiftUI WindowGroup chosen for native macOS look; replaces CustomTkinter app.py; does not replicate Telegram chat or MCP server management

nodes.label: macOS Accessibility Permission
nodes.contributor: rationale: Required for AX tree, click, type, scroll, window control and pynput input injection; fails silently if not granted

nodes.label: macOS Screen Recording Permission
nodes.contributor: rationale: Required for screenshot/snapshot tools; CGWindowListCreateImage returns nil if not granted

nodes.label: AX Tree Traversal Performance (Mach IPC bottleneck)
nodes.contributor: rationale: AX calls cross process boundary via Mach IPC vs Windows in-process COM TreeWalker; 2-5x slower; mitigated by depth limit (8), ThreadPoolExecutor per window, aggressive AXUIElement caching, and batch attribute reads via AXUIElementCopyMultipleAttributeValues
nodes.norm_label: ax tree traversal performance (mach ipc bottleneck)

nodes.label: Safari Playwright Limitation
nodes.contributor: rationale: playwright does not support Safari; DOM extraction falls back to AX tree only for Safari; Chrome and Firefox use playwright as on Windows

nodes.norm_label: lotus banner image (banner.png) — panoramic pink lotus pond painting

nodes.norm_label: lotus logo (lotus_logo.png) — hot pink geometric lotus mandala

nodes.label: Mac App Icon (mac_app_icon.png) — Lotus logo on rounded white square (macOS app icon style)
nodes.norm_label: mac app icon (mac_app_icon.png) — lotus logo on rounded white square (macos app icon style)

nodes.label: Screenshot 1 — Claude Desktop showing Windows-MCP tool list
nodes.contributor: rationale: Shows MCP tools visible in Claude Desktop: Launch-Tool, Powershell-Tool, State-Tool, Clipboard-Tool, Click-Tool, Type-Tool, Switch-Tool, Scroll-Tool — these are the Windows-MCP tools being ported to Mac-MCP
nodes.norm_label: screenshot 1 — claude desktop showing windows-mcp tool list

nodes.label: Screenshot 2 — Claude automating Notepad via Windows-MCP (type paragraph about LLMs)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Demonstrates tool chain: State-Tool (capture window state) → Launch-Tool (open Notepad) → State-Tool (verify) → Type-Tool (type LLM paragraph); shows end-to-end desktop automation workflow
nodes.norm_label: screenshot 2 — claude automating notepad via windows-mcp (type paragraph about llms)

nodes.label: Screenshot 3 — Claude searching weather in Kochi via browser automation
nodes.contributor: rationale: Demonstrates cross-tool workflow: Launch-Tool (open Edge) → State-Tool (observe browser) → Click-Tool (address bar) → Type-Tool (search query) → Enter; result shows Google weather card for Kochi at 28C
nodes.norm_label: screenshot 3 — claude searching weather in kochi via browser automation

nodes.label: Mac-MCP Environment Variables
nodes.contributor: rationale: Renamed from WINDOWS_MCP_* prefix to MAC_MCP_* prefix; controls screenshot backend, scale factor, snapshot profiling, debug mode, telemetry

nodes.label: Batch AX Attribute Reads (AXUIElementCopyMultipleAttributeValues)
nodes.contributor: rationale: Fetches role, title, value, description, position, size, enabled, hidden, children in one Mach IPC call instead of 9 separate calls; primary mitigation for AX traversal latency

links.source_location: Shows Powershell-Tool and Windows-style tool names
Both are command execution backends in the same file; shell for filesystem/process, AppleScript for app/window control
Both use subprocess to invoke CLI tools (defaults, bash) for system-level operations
Demonstrates browser automation via Click-Tool, Type-Tool
banner image shown in setup wizard
cursor/click icon visually represents desktop automation purpose
mac_app_icon uses same lotus geometric design as lotus_logo
